user_message: Working on Parnell Aerospace's QBR preparation. Got the support ticket -- help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-02-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Parnell Aerospace)

[00:01:15] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:17:00] David (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:22:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-M trap (Separation of security-posture-value from migration-architecture forces awkward ); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-M failure mode (Separation of security-posture-value from migration-architecture forces awkward )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-M -- Separation of security-posture-value from migration-architecture forces awkward
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
